title: Supreme Court issues notices to Centre on PIL seeking ban on employment of children in orchestras, spas

title: As tech hiring hits a pause
description: The demand for the Indian tech talent, predominantly concentrated in Bengaluru, seems to be on a downward slope. Once a lucrative sector for aspirational young Indians, there is a lull in hiring in the ITeS sector. The Hindu speaks to experts who say the shift is structural not cyclical

title: Assam tables UCC Bill; proposes ban on polygamy
description: Assam has introduced the Uniform Civil Code Bill, 2026, in the State Assembly, becoming the latest State to move towards a UCC. The Bill proposes a ban on polygamy, mandatory registration of live-in relationships, and changes to laws governing marriage, divorce and succession. If passed, Assam will become the third State after Uttarakhand and Gujarat to enact a Uniform Civil Code.

title: Caste-iron control: The threat of Rajasthan’s caste panchayats
description: Extrajudicial panchayats in Rajasthan use the age-old tool of social ostracisation combined with extortionary fines to control communities. Sreeparna Chakrabarty reports on how the Rajasthan High Court has taken note of this parallel justice system within each caste, the victims’ plight, and lack of a law that targets the social evil

The film currently sits just Rs 2.42 crore away from crossing the monumental Rs 1150 crore net domestic mark in India. description: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge has strongly criticized the Modi government for recent fuel price increases, alleging it burdens citizens despite stable international crude oil rates. He pointed to significant hikes in petrol and diesel prices over the past decade, contrasting them with the UPA era.

description: The World Health Organization's Kyiv office sustained damage from debris during recent Russian strikes, prompting the WHO Director-General to renew calls for a ceasefire. The building, housing multiple UN agencies, saw shattered windows but no injuries. These attacks, which killed at least four civilians and injured others, follow Moscow's vow of retaliation for Ukrainian strikes.

description: A shocking land dispute turned deadly in Ambala as a 22-year-old allegedly shot his elder brother, grandmother, and uncle dead, critically injuring his aunt.  The accused, Abhishek, reportedly opened fire during a heated argument over sand sale proceeds.  Police are actively searching for him and investigating the source of the weapon.

Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system
description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Shaun Romy speaks about Mohanlal's 'Drishyam 3' alongside 'Karakkam' release
description: Actress Shaun Romy expresses excitement for Malayalam cinema's festive season, highlighting the contrasting releases of 'Drishyam 3' and her upcoming musical horror-comedy 'Karakkam'. She praises Mohanlal's inspiring presence and shares details about 'Karakkam', a light-hearted supernatural comedy with a strong musical element. Meanwhile, 'Drishyam 3' continues its impressive worldwide box office run.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
description: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.
